regions.title: Bile acid synthesis (classic and alternative pathways)
Short-chain fatty acid production by gut microbiota
Warburg effect and cancer metabolic rewiring
Glutathione synthesis and redox cycling (γ-glutamyl cycle)
Sphingolipid metabolism (ceramide and the S1P rheostat)

reactions.enzymeName: Pyruvate dehydrogenase complex, E1 component α (PDH)
Trifunctional enzyme subunit alpha (MTPα; LCEH + LCHAD)
Trifunctional enzyme subunit alpha (MTPα; LCEH + LCHAD)
Pyruvate dehydrogenase E1 component subunit alpha (somatic)
Pyruvate dehydrogenase E1 component subunit alpha (somatic)
Ubiquinol:cytochrome c oxidoreductase / cytochrome bc1 (Complex III)
Serine palmitoyltransferase long chain base subunit 2
Multifunctional protein CAD (CPS II · aspartate transcarbamoylase · dihydroorotase)
Multifunctional protein CAD (CPS II · aspartate transcarbamoylase · dihydroorotase)
Multifunctional protein CAD (CPS II · aspartate transcarbamoylase · dihydroorotase)

connectors.label: Cholesterol biosynthesis (mevalonate pathway)
connectors.note: Cholesterol, the obligate substrate for all bile-acid synthesis, is supplied by de novo cholesterol biosynthesis and lipoprotein-derived cholesterol; bile-acid synthesis is the major route of cholesterol catabolism/elimination.

connectors.label: Glycolysis (Embden–Meyerhof–Parnas)
connectors.note: Microbial glycolysis (Embden-Meyerhof-Parnas) of dietary-fibre–derived hexoses supplies the pyruvate/PEP that feeds all three SCFA branches.

connectors.label: Citric acid cycle (TCA / Krebs cycle)
connectors.note: Acetate-derived acetyl-CoA is oxidised in the host TCA cycle.

connectors.label: Citric acid cycle (TCA / Krebs cycle)
connectors.note: The succinate propionate pathway runs the reductive TCA branch (OAA-malate-fumarate-succinate-succinyl-CoA), sharing intermediates with the host citric-acid cycle.

connectors.label: Citric acid cycle (TCA / Krebs cycle)
connectors.note: Propionyl-CoA from valine and isoleucine is carboxylated by propionyl-CoA carboxylase and isomerised (via methylmalonyl-CoA, MUT) to succinyl-CoA — an anaplerotic, glucogenic entry into the TCA cycle.

connectors.label: Citric acid cycle (TCA / Krebs cycle)
connectors.note: Acetyl-CoA generated from leucine and isoleucine is oxidised in the TCA cycle.

connectors.label: De novo pyrimidine nucleotide synthesis
connectors.note: Thymidylate synthase provides the sole de novo route to dTMP, linking the folate pool to pyrimidine/DNA synthesis.

connectors.label: De novo purine nucleotide biosynthesis
connectors.note: 10-formyl-THF donates purine ring carbons C8 (GART) and C2 (ATIC) during de novo purine synthesis.

connectors.label: Glycine, Serine and Threonine Metabolism
connectors.note: Serine and glycine, the one-carbon donors, are supplied by serine/glycine metabolism (serine itself is made de novo from the glycolytic intermediate 3-phosphoglycerate).

connectors.label: Glycolysis (Embden–Meyerhof–Parnas)
connectors.note: Glycolytic pyruvate is imported into the mitochondrion and oxidized by the PDH complex to the acetyl-CoA that feeds the cycle.

connectors.label: Oxidative phosphorylation (electron transport chain)
connectors.note: NADH from the three NAD+-linked dehydrogenases donates electrons to Complex I of the respiratory chain.

connectors.label: De novo fatty acid synthesis (palmitate)
connectors.note: Citrate exported to the cytosol is cleaved by ATP-citrate lyase to supply acetyl-CoA for fatty-acid and sterol synthesis (citrate shuttle).

connectors.label: Glycolysis (Embden–Meyerhof–Parnas)
connectors.note: Cytosolic citrate is an allosteric inhibitor of phosphofructokinase-1, slowing glycolysis when biosynthetic precursors are abundant.

connectors.label: Glutamate and glutamine metabolism
connectors.note: 2-Oxoglutarate and glutamate interconvert by transamination and glutamate dehydrogenase, a major anaplerotic and nitrogen-handling node.

connectors.label: Gluconeogenesis (glucose synthesis from pyruvate)
connectors.note: Oxaloacetate is withdrawn (cataplerosis) and converted by PEPCK to phosphoenolpyruvate to begin gluconeogenesis.

connectors.label: Heme biosynthesis (porphyrin pathway)
connectors.note: Succinyl-CoA condenses with glycine (ALA synthase) as the entry step of porphyrin and heme biosynthesis.

connectors.label: Fatty acid β-oxidation (mitochondrial)
connectors.note: Beta-oxidation and ketone-body catabolism feed acetyl-CoA into the cycle alongside carbohydrate-derived acetyl-CoA.

connectors.label: Urea cycle (Krebs–Henseleit ornithine cycle)
connectors.note: Fumarate released by the argininosuccinate lyase step of the urea cycle joins the cellular fumarate pool (aspartate-argininosuccinate shunt).

connectors.label: Pentose phosphate pathway (hexose monophosphate shunt)
connectors.note: Ribose-5-phosphate, the backbone precursor (via PRPP), is supplied largely by the pentose phosphate pathway.

connectors.label: Purine salvage pathway
connectors.note: IMP, AMP and GMP are also produced from free bases by the purine salvage pathway (HGPRT/APRT), which spares the energetically costly de novo route.

connectors.label: Glycolysis (Embden–Meyerhof–Parnas)
connectors.note: Serine synthesis branches from the glycolytic intermediate 3-phosphoglycerate, diverting glycolytic carbon into amino-acid and one-carbon metabolism.

connectors.label: Glycolysis (Embden–Meyerhof–Parnas)
connectors.note: L-Serine is an allosteric activator of pyruvate kinase M2 (PKM2); serine depletion lowers PKM2 activity, feeding carbon back toward serine synthesis.

connectors.label: De novo pyrimidine nucleotide synthesis
connectors.note: 5,10-Methylene-THF from SHMT and glycine cleavage is the one-carbon donor for thymidylate synthase (dUMP -> dTMP) — the folate one-carbon pool entry point.

connectors.label: De novo purine nucleotide biosynthesis
connectors.note: Serine/glycine-derived one-carbon units (via 10-formyl-THF) supply C2 and C8 of the purine ring.

connectors.label: De novo purine nucleotide biosynthesis
connectors.note: Glycine is incorporated intact into the purine ring (contributes C4, C5 and N7).

connectors.label: Glutathione synthesis and redox cycling (γ-glutamyl cycle)
connectors.note: Glycine is the C-terminal residue of glutathione (gamma-Glu-Cys-Gly).

connectors.label: Sphingolipid metabolism (ceramide and the S1P rheostat)
connectors.note: Serine palmitoyltransferase condenses L-serine with palmitoyl-CoA to initiate de novo sphingolipid synthesis.

connectors.label: Glycolysis (Embden–Meyerhof–Parnas)
connectors.note: PFKFB3-derived fructose-2,6-bisphosphate is the most potent allosteric activator of PFK-1, accelerating flux through the glycolysis module (opposed by p53/TIGAR).

connectors.label: Pentose phosphate pathway (hexose monophosphate shunt)
connectors.note: Glucose-6-phosphate is diverted into the oxidative PPP for NADPH and ribose-5-phosphate; PKM2's bottleneck and TIGAR both promote this shunt to support proliferation and redox balance.

connectors.label: Glycine, Serine and Threonine Metabolism
connectors.note: PHGDH branches 3-phosphoglycerate into serine and one-carbon metabolism, supplying nucleotides, glutathione and methylation capacity.

connectors.label: Citric acid cycle (TCA / Krebs cycle)
connectors.note: Pyruvate's oxidative fate via PDH feeds the TCA cycle; this route is actively suppressed by PDK1 in the Warburg state.

connectors.label: Citric acid cycle (TCA / Krebs cycle)
connectors.note: Glutamine-derived α-ketoglutarate replenishes TCA-cycle carbon (anaplerosis), the mainstay of glutamine addiction.

connectors.label: Glutamate and glutamine metabolism
connectors.note: Tumor glutaminolysis draws on the systemic glutamine pool detailed in the glutamate–glutamine module.

connectors.label: Gluconeogenesis (glucose synthesis from pyruvate)
connectors.note: Lactate exported by tumors can be recycled to glucose in the liver (Cori cycle), a systemic feature of cancer cachexia.

connectors.label: Citric acid cycle (TCA / Krebs cycle)
connectors.note: Each spiral turn releases acetyl-CoA, which is oxidized to CO2 in the TCA cycle.

connectors.label: Ketone body metabolism (ketogenesis / ketolysis)
connectors.note: In fasting liver, β-oxidation-derived acetyl-CoA is diverted to ketone-body synthesis.

connectors.label: De novo fatty acid synthesis (palmitate)
connectors.note: Malonyl-CoA, made by acetyl-CoA carboxylase in fatty-acid synthesis, is the key inhibitor of CPT1 — the node that reciprocally coordinates synthesis and oxidation.

connectors.label: Glycolysis (Embden–Meyerhof–Parnas)
connectors.note: Glucose–fatty-acid (Randle) cycle: acetyl-CoA and NADH from β-oxidation inhibit pyruvate dehydrogenase and (via citrate) PFK-1, sparing glucose when fatty acids are plentiful.

connectors.label: Citric acid cycle (TCA / Krebs cycle)
connectors.note: 2-Oxoglutarate is a TCA-cycle intermediate; glutamate/glutamine are its principal anaplerotic source (glutaminolysis) and cataplerotic sink.

connectors.label: Citric acid cycle (TCA / Krebs cycle)
connectors.note: Aspartate aminotransferase couples glutamate/2-oxoglutarate to oxaloacetate, the entry point of the TCA cycle and of the malate–aspartate shuttle.

connectors.label: Glycolysis (Embden–Meyerhof–Parnas)
connectors.note: Alanine aminotransferase links glutamate metabolism to pyruvate in the glucose–alanine cycle and gluconeogenesis.

connectors.label: Glycolysis (Embden–Meyerhof–Parnas)
connectors.note: GFPT1 siphons fructose-6-phosphate out of glycolysis into hexosamine biosynthesis, using the glutamine amide nitrogen.

connectors.label: Urea cycle (Krebs–Henseleit ornithine cycle)
connectors.note: N-Acetylglutamate is the obligate allosteric activator of carbamoyl-phosphate synthetase 1, switching on the urea cycle.

connectors.label: Urea cycle (Krebs–Henseleit ornithine cycle)
connectors.note: Transaminase-generated aspartate donates the second nitrogen atom of urea via argininosuccinate synthesis.

connectors.label: Glutathione synthesis and redox cycling (γ-glutamyl cycle)
connectors.note: γ-Glutamylcysteine from GCLC is the committed precursor that glutathione synthetase converts to glutathione.

connectors.label: De novo pyrimidine nucleotide synthesis
connectors.note: Glutamine supplies the amide nitrogen for carbamoyl phosphate (CAD / CPS II) in de novo pyrimidine biosynthesis.

connectors.label: Glycolysis (Embden–Meyerhof–Parnas)
connectors.note: Pyruvate is the end product of glycolysis; it is carried into the matrix by the mitochondrial pyruvate carrier before oxidation by PDC.

connectors.label: Citric acid cycle (TCA / Krebs cycle)
connectors.note: Acetyl-CoA condenses with oxaloacetate (citrate synthase) to enter the TCA cycle — the principal fate of PDC-derived acetyl units.

connectors.label: Oxidative phosphorylation (electron transport chain)
connectors.note: NADH produced by E3 is reoxidized at Complex I of the electron transport chain, coupling PDC flux to ATP synthesis.

connectors.label: De novo fatty acid synthesis (palmitate)
connectors.note: PDC is the main source of lipogenic acetyl-CoA, which is exported to the cytosol as citrate for de novo fatty-acid synthesis.

connectors.label: Ketone body metabolism (ketogenesis / ketolysis)
connectors.note: Acetyl-CoA is the substrate for hepatic ketogenesis when it exceeds TCA-cycle capacity.

connectors.label: Gluconeogenesis (glucose synthesis from pyruvate)
connectors.note: Reciprocal control during fasting: acetyl-CoA allosterically activates pyruvate carboxylase, diverting pyruvate to oxaloacetate/gluconeogenesis while PDK4 keeps PDC switched off.

connectors.label: Citric acid cycle (TCA / Krebs cycle)
connectors.note: Cytosolic acetyl-CoA for sterol synthesis derives from mitochondrial citrate exported and cleaved by ATP-citrate lyase, linking cholesterol synthesis to the TCA cycle.

connectors.label: Citric acid cycle (TCA / Krebs cycle)
connectors.note: The TCA cycle is the principal source of the matrix NADH oxidized by Complex I; the two pathways are tightly coupled through the matrix NADH/NAD+ pool.

connectors.label: Citric acid cycle (TCA / Krebs cycle)
connectors.note: Complex II IS succinate dehydrogenase — the only membrane-embedded TCA-cycle enzyme — physically linking the citric-acid cycle to the respiratory chain through the succinate/fumarate couple.

connectors.label: Glycolysis (Embden–Meyerhof–Parnas)
connectors.note: Cytosolic NADH produced by glycolysis is delivered to the chain indirectly through the malate–aspartate and glycerol-3-phosphate shuttles, which regenerate cytosolic NAD+ and feed electrons to Complex I or the ubiquinone pool.

connectors.label: Glycolysis (Embden–Meyerhof–Parnas)
connectors.note: Muscle glycogenolysis feeds glucose 6-phosphate directly into glycolysis, bypassing the hexokinase ATP cost.

connectors.label: Gluconeogenesis (glucose synthesis from pyruvate)
connectors.note: Gluconeogenic glucose 6-phosphate can be routed into glycogen; both pathways share glucose-6-phosphatase for hepatic glucose output.

connectors.label: Pentose phosphate pathway (hexose monophosphate shunt)
connectors.note: Glucose 6-phosphate is the shared entry metabolite of the pentose phosphate pathway.

connectors.label: Glycolysis (Embden–Meyerhof–Parnas)
connectors.note: Hepatic glucose-6-phosphatase releases free glucose to the blood, fueling glycolysis in peripheral tissues.

connectors.label: Cholesterol biosynthesis (mevalonate pathway)
connectors.note: Acetoacetyl-CoA and HMG-CoA are shared with the cytosolic mevalonate/cholesterol pathway (via HMGCS1 and HMG-CoA reductase).

connectors.label: Citric acid cycle (TCA / Krebs cycle)
connectors.note: In extrahepatic tissues, ketolysis-derived acetyl-CoA enters the TCA cycle for terminal oxidation.

connectors.label: Citric acid cycle (TCA / Krebs cycle)
connectors.note: SCOT draws succinyl-CoA from the TCA cycle to activate acetoacetate, coupling ketolysis to the succinyl-CoA/succinate node (bypassing succinyl-CoA synthetase).

connectors.label: Citric acid cycle (TCA / Krebs cycle)
connectors.note: Fumarate from ASL enters the TCA cycle (aspartate–argininosuccinate shunt), where it is oxidized to oxaloacetate and transaminated back to aspartate.

connectors.label: Citric acid cycle (TCA / Krebs cycle)
connectors.note: Aspartate, the second-nitrogen donor consumed by ASS1, is regenerated from TCA-cycle oxaloacetate by aspartate aminotransferase (GOT2).

connectors.label: Gluconeogenesis (glucose synthesis from pyruvate)
connectors.note: Fumarate → malate → oxaloacetate feeds hepatic gluconeogenesis, co-localized with ureagenesis in periportal hepatocytes.

connectors.label: Glutamate and glutamine metabolism
connectors.note: Glutamate (and glycine) precursors for glutathione synthesis are drawn from amino-acid metabolism; glutamate is also released back to that pool when GGT degrades glutathione.

connectors.label: Glycolysis (Embden–Meyerhof–Parnas)
connectors.note: Methylglyoxal is a toxic dicarbonyl formed from the glycolytic triose phosphates (DHAP/glyceraldehyde-3-phosphate); the glutathione-dependent glyoxalase system is its principal detoxification route.

connectors.label: Oxidative phosphorylation (electron transport chain)
connectors.note: Electron leak from the mitochondrial respiratory chain generates superoxide/H2O2; the glutathione peroxidase–reductase couple is a primary scavenger of this reactive oxygen species.

connectors.label: Pentose phosphate pathway (hexose monophosphate shunt)
connectors.note: G6P is the branch point into the pentose phosphate pathway.

connectors.label: Citric acid cycle (TCA / Krebs cycle)
connectors.note: Pyruvate feeds the TCA cycle via pyruvate dehydrogenase.

connectors.label: Glycine, Serine and Threonine Metabolism
connectors.note: L-serine supplied by serine/glycine metabolism is condensed with palmitoyl-CoA at the committed SPT step, coupling serine availability to sphingolipid synthesis.

connectors.label: De novo fatty acid synthesis (palmitate)
connectors.note: Palmitoyl-CoA from de novo lipogenesis / fatty-acid activation is the acyl donor for SPT, and long-chain acyl-CoAs feed the ceramide synthases, linking lipogenesis to sphingolipid supply.

connectors.label: Glycolysis (Embden–Meyerhof–Parnas)
connectors.note: Gluconeogenesis is the biosynthetic opposite of glycolysis: they share seven near-equilibrium enzymes (steps 6-13 here) but gluconeogenesis bypasses the three irreversible glycolytic steps with four enzymes (pyruvate carboxylase + PEPCK, FBPase-1, glucose-6-phosphatase). The two are reciprocally regulated to avoid a futile ATP-wasting cycle.

connectors.label: Citric acid cycle (TCA / Krebs cycle)
connectors.note: Oxaloacetate is shared with the TCA cycle: anaplerotic OAA (from pyruvate carboxylase) and glucogenic amino acids entering the cycle are withdrawn (cataplerosis) toward glucose, and mitochondrial GTP for PEPCK is supplied by the succinyl-CoA synthetase step.

connectors.label: Pentose phosphate pathway (hexose monophosphate shunt)
connectors.note: Glucose 6-phosphate made by gluconeogenesis can be routed into the pentose phosphate pathway for NADPH and ribose 5-phosphate instead of being dephosphorylated to free glucose.

connectors.label: De novo purine nucleotide biosynthesis
connectors.note: IMP is the branch-point hub shared with de novo purine biosynthesis; salvage regenerates IMP directly from hypoxanthine, bypassing the energetically costly 10-step de novo route, and both feed the shared IMP-to-AMP and IMP-to-GMP branches.

connectors.label: Citric acid cycle (TCA / Krebs cycle)
connectors.note: In the purine nucleotide cycle (AMP -> IMP -> adenylosuccinate -> AMP), the ADSL step releases fumarate, an anaplerotic input to the TCA cycle that is quantitatively important in exercising skeletal muscle.

connectors.label: Glycolysis (Embden–Meyerhof–Parnas)
connectors.note: Glucose 6-phosphate is the shared entry metabolite: hexokinase-derived G6P from glycolysis is drawn into the oxidative PPP.

connectors.label: Glycolysis (Embden–Meyerhof–Parnas)
connectors.note: The non-oxidative branch returns carbon to glycolysis as fructose 6-phosphate via transaldolase and transketolase.

connectors.label: Glycolysis (Embden–Meyerhof–Parnas)
connectors.note: The non-oxidative branch returns carbon to glycolysis as glyceraldehyde 3-phosphate; conversely, glycolytic G3P/F6P can feed the branch in reverse to make ribose.

connectors.label: De novo fatty acid synthesis (palmitate)
connectors.note: PPP-derived NADPH is the principal reductant for fatty-acid and sterol biosynthesis in liver and adipose tissue.

connectors.label: Glutathione synthesis and redox cycling (γ-glutamyl cycle)
connectors.note: NADPH regenerates reduced glutathione via glutathione reductase, the main defense against oxidative stress — critical in erythrocytes lacking mitochondria.

connectors.label: Glutathione synthesis and redox cycling (γ-glutamyl cycle)
connectors.note: Transsulfuration-derived cysteine is the rate-limiting precursor of glutathione, linking sulfur-amino-acid supply to cellular antioxidant capacity.

connectors.label: Pentose phosphate pathway (hexose monophosphate shunt)
connectors.note: PRPP, the activated ribose-5-phosphate donor used at step 5 and the key CPS II activator, is generated by PRPP synthetase from ribose-5-phosphate of the pentose phosphate pathway.

connectors.label: Urea cycle (Krebs–Henseleit ornithine cycle)
connectors.note: Carbamoyl phosphate is shared with the urea cycle, but the pools are separate: cytosolic CPS II (CAD) uses glutamine for pyrimidines, whereas mitochondrial CPS I uses ammonia for the urea cycle.

connectors.label: De novo purine nucleotide biosynthesis
connectors.note: PRPP is the shared committed precursor of both purine and pyrimidine nucleotide synthesis, and GTP activation of CTP synthase further coordinates the two branches.

connectors.label: Glycogen synthesis and breakdown (glycogenesis & glycogenolysis)
connectors.note: UTP activates glucose-1-phosphate to UDP-glucose, the glucosyl donor for glycogen and glycoconjugate synthesis.

connectors.label: Citric acid cycle (TCA / Krebs cycle)
connectors.note: Citrate produced by the TCA cycle is exported from the mitochondrion to supply cytosolic acetyl-CoA for lipogenesis (the citrate shuttle).

connectors.label: Glycolysis (Embden–Meyerhof–Parnas)
connectors.note: Glucose-derived pyruvate from glycolysis is oxidatively decarboxylated to the mitochondrial acetyl-CoA that becomes cytosolic citrate and lipogenic carbon.

connectors.label: Oxidative phosphorylation (electron transport chain)
connectors.note: Heme b is the prosthetic group of the respiratory-chain cytochromes (Complex II heme b, Complex III hemes bL/bH/c1, cytochrome c) and the biosynthetic precursor of the heme a of Complex IV, so heme biosynthesis provisions oxidative phosphorylation.
